subject: Re: Import into M, one round with options off -> 650A
body:
Hi Joe,

That sounds like it could be an incorrect pixel size. If the pixel size you
enter for the star file after downgrading is incorrect then it may just be
working with noise, although I'm not sure if that would explain why it
reextracts with no problems afterwards. The most recent version of Warp/M
now supports Relion 3.1 star files and as such you shouldn't need to use
the relion_star_downgrade.

Also, does your mask look reasonable? Was it generated in M or imported
from something like Relion?

Cheers,

Mat


On Tue, 10 Oct 2023, 09:22 joe dobbs, <dobbsjoseph12@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi,
>
> I have a strange issue and wondered if any of you have seen similar:
>
> -refinement in relion of a ribosome reaches 20A
> -import into M (using relion_star_downgrade from dynamo2m)
> -one iteration with all options turned off to test the import went ok ->
> 650A empty looking structure (+M crashes)
> (as suggested in
> https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/ZMr-uW8bBzU/m/CweU9wrxCgAJ)
> -however, re-extract of the particles from M directly after import and
> relion_reconstruct -> ribosome is still there
>
> I don't use the pre-rotate particles option, and the star file conversion
> seems to have gone ok- these potential sources of error are indicated
> against since I can again reconstruct my structure without realignment
> after M export.
>
> It is then very strange that one iteration without any options affected
> the structure in any way- surely it should have just come back with my 20A
> ribosome, not some 650A thing.
>
> Has anyone seen anything like this?
>
> --
